text.title: Agentic User Story Mapping Workflow
text.summary: Six-subagent pipeline from flat backlog to release-ready slices, model selection rationale, and the XML prompt pattern for each agent role.

section.title: Six-Agent Pipeline

rule.statement: Pipeline: backlog-ingester (haiku) → persona-resolver (sonnet) → activity-extractor (sonnet) → task-grouper (sonnet) → story-rewriter (sonnet) → release-slicer (opus). Each stage is gated by BA review before the next starts. Jira-syncer (haiku) runs on BA approval after release-slicer.
rule.rationale: Haiku for ingestion and sync (pattern matching, API calls). Sonnet for structured authoring (personas, activities, tasks, stories). Opus only for release slicing — the only cross-cutting reasoning task where capacity, journey completeness, and outcome trade off simultaneously.

rule.statement: Every agent emits YAML diffs against the current map.yaml, never full-tree rewrites. BA reviews diffs in PR form. Full rewrites are rejected because they obscure what changed and prevent conflict detection.
rule.rationale: Diff-based updates preserve the BA's ability to review and reject individual changes. Full rewrites conflate unrelated changes and cannot be partially accepted.

rule.statement: Release-slicer (opus) must include backbone-coverage as a hard constraint: R1 must touch at least N-1 backbone activities where N is the total count. Capacity feasibility (story-points sum / velocity × sprints <= 1.0) is also a hard constraint, not a soft guideline.
rule.rationale: Opus optimizes for capacity over journey integrity by default. Hard constraints on backbone coverage and capacity feasibility prevent the two most common release-slicer failures.

section.reference: Structured XML agent prompt covering backbone rules, orphan handling, capacity feasibility, and diff-only output.
Python script that syncs map.yaml to Jira: creates epics per activity, stories per leaf, labels per release and task.

rule.statement: Run the coverage-auditor (sonnet) weekly: compare story map backbone activities against the current Jira state and emit a coverage matrix with tasks without stories and stories without tasks. Re-digitize workshop output within 24 hours.
rule.rationale: Maps go stale within 2 sprints if not owned. Weekly drift checks catch divergence before it accumulates into a rebuild. Workshop output not digitized within 24 hours is effectively lost.

rule.statement: Cap activity-extractor and task-grouper at 80k context tokens. Cap release-slicer at 200k context tokens for the full map plus capacity plus dependency graph. If the map exceeds these limits, chunk by sub-journey (call activity) and stitch in a final orchestrator pass.
rule.rationale: Unbounded context in the release-slicer produces diluted attention on capacity and dependency constraints. The 200k cap forces chunking that keeps constraint reasoning sharp.
